title: Google Ads Gambling Policy Expansion and Crackdown in US 2026
summary: Google expanded gambling ads policy Jan 2026 to include federally regulated Prediction Markets. March 23, 2026 update requires 'Good Policy Health' with historical compliance checks; discrepancies in licenses reject certifications. US iGaming needs unbroken ownership chain proof.

title: New York AI Disclosure Law for Advertising Effective 2026
summary: New York 2025 law effective June 9 requires conspicuous disclosure of AI-generated 'synthetic performers' in ads, including online/social for iGaming. FTC 'Operation AI Comply' enforced 12+ actions by early 2026 on AI claims.

title: AGA Responsible Gaming Code of Conduct 2026 with CCRB Oversight
summary: American Gaming Association's 2026 Code mandates compliance for gaming ads/marketing by members and agents. Code Compliance Review Board oversees digital ads, geolocation verification, content moderation, internal reviews for responsible gaming.

title: European iGaming Ad Fragmentation: Italy Fines and Spain Bans 2025-2026
summary: 2025-2026: Italy fined global platforms for non-compliant gambling ads. Spain pursues near-total bans; Baltic states prepare stricter laws on sports/digital branding. Regulators target full affiliate chain compliance across markets.

title: New Jersey S2356 Mandates Rotating Responsible Gaming Phrases 2026
summary: Early 2026, New Jersey S2356 bill requires 3-10 specific 'Responsible Gaming' phrases to rotate in all digital gambling ads. Google Ads demands IRS docs matching licenses exactly; refresh creatives every 30 days.

title: Platform Tightened Policies: Google, Meta, TikTok iGaming Ads 2026
summary: 2026: Google certifies real-money gambling in UK/Spain/Italy with licenses, age gates. Meta needs permission, no minors/'get-rich' claims. TikTok pilots licensed regions with strict age/compliance controls; all require responsible gaming links.

title: Global Gambling Ad Rules: Australia IGA/ACMA Enforcement 2026 Guide
summary: 2026 guide: Australia's Interactive Gambling Act/ACMA bans most ads; Google bans offline gambling ads in 35 countries. Requires licenses, no minor targeting, age-gating, responsible info on landings; market-specific legal reviews essential.
